title: Venezuelan Coco Sugar Crumble
description: A rich, comforting, crumbly dessert featuring the sweetness of coco sugar and the spiciness of ferment-jang. Perfect for a sick-day treat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, cream together butter and coco sugar until light and fluffy.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flours, baking powder, yeast, and salt.
Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture and stir until a crumbly dough forms.
Gradually add granulated sugar, eggs, milk, and vanilla extract, stirring until a smooth batter forms.
Pour the batter into a greased 9x9-inch baking dish.
Bake for 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
While the crumble is baking, mix ferment-jang with a little water to create a paste.
Once the crumble is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Drizzle the warm crumble with the ferment-jang paste and serve.
Let the crumble cool completely before refrigerating or freezing.
